document.name: pr-review
document.title: PR Review
document.summary: Sweep / GitHub Copilot Coding Agent review shape. Fetch a PR's metadata + files-changed via the GitHub piece (uses the project's GitHub OAuth connection), run a reviewer agent over the summary, produce a markdown review with a parsed VERDICT line, and optionally post it as a single PR comment. Persists the review as a workflow_artifact (slot=primary) so it renders front-and-centre on the run-detail Overview tab.

repoOwner.title: Repository owner
repoOwner.description: GitHub user or org that owns the repo (e.g. 'PittampalliOrg').

focus.title: Optional review focus
focus.description: Appended verbatim to the reviewer prompt. E.g. 'security implications of the new env var' or 'performance under load'.

postComment.title: Post as PR comment
postComment.description: When true, posts the review as a single PR comment via the GitHub API.

githubConnection.title: GitHub connection external_id
githubConnection.description: The app_connection external_id for your project's GitHub connection (looks like `conn_...`). Find it in /workspaces/<slug>/connections.

input.query: query ($owner: String!, $repo: String!, $number: Int!) { repository(owner: $owner, name: $repo) { pullRequest(number: $number) { title body baseRefName headRefName mergeable additions deletions changedFiles files(first: 100) { nodes { path additions deletions changeType } } commits(last: 5) { nodes { commit { messageHeadline messageBody } } } } } }

body.prompt:
${ "You are reviewing pull request #" + (.trigger.prNumber | tostring) + " on https://github.com/" + .trigger.repoOwner + "/" + .trigger.repoName + ".

PR TITLE: " + (.fetch_pr.body.title // "(no title)") + "
PR AUTHOR: " + (.fetch_pr.body.user.login // "unknown") + "
BASE BRANCH: " + (.fetch_files.body.data.repository.pullRequest.baseRefName // "main") + "
HEAD BRANCH: " + (.fetch_files.body.data.repository.pullRequest.headRefName // "") + "
MERGEABLE: " + (.fetch_files.body.data.repository.pullRequest.mergeable // "UNKNOWN") + "
CHANGES: +" + ((.fetch_files.body.data.repository.pullRequest.additions // 0) | tostring) + " / -" + ((.fetch_files.body.data.repository.pullRequest.deletions // 0) | tostring) + " across " + ((.fetch_files.body.data.repository.pullRequest.changedFiles // 0) | tostring) + " file(s).

PR DESCRIPTION:
" + (.fetch_pr.body.body // "(no description)") + "

FILES CHANGED:
" + ((.fetch_files.body.data.repository.pullRequest.files.nodes // []) | map("- " + .path + "  (+" + (.additions|tostring) + " / -" + (.deletions|tostring) + ", " + .changeType + ")") | join("
")) + "

RECENT COMMITS:
" + ((.fetch_files.body.data.repository.pullRequest.commits.nodes // []) | map("- " + .commit.messageHeadline) | join("
")) + "

This is a READ-ONLY review. DO NOT modify, create, or delete any files. You only have the metadata + files-changed summary above (no line-by-line diff).

Produce a markdown review with these sections:

1. **Summary** — one paragraph: what this PR appears to do, based on title/body/file list.
2. **Risks & concerns** — issues you can infer from the file list and commit messages: high-risk file paths touched (auth, migrations, secrets handling), suspiciously large changes, missing tests for changed code. If none, say `(none)`.
3. **Style & maintainability** — observations from the file list (e.g. inconsistent naming, churn in one area).
4. **Suggested follow-ups** — non-blocking improvements.

End with EXACTLY one final line:
`VERDICT: approve` | `VERDICT: request-changes` | `VERDICT: comment-only`" + (if (.trigger.focus // "") != "" then "

ADDITIONAL FOCUS REQUESTED BY REVIEWER: " + .trigger.focus else "" end) }

artifacts.title: PR review
artifacts.metadata: ${ { repoOwner: .trigger.repoOwner, repoName: .trigger.repoName, prNumber: .trigger.prNumber, prTitle: .fetch_pr.body.title, prAuthor: .fetch_pr.body.user.login, focus: (.trigger.focus // ""), turn: .data.turn, model: .data.modelSpec } }

as.verdict: ${ ((.review_step.data.content // "") | split("\n") | map(select(startswith("VERDICT:"))) | first // "VERDICT: comment-only") | split(":") | .[1:] | join(":") | ltrimstr(" ") | split("\r") | first }
